sglang.title: Memory pool fix for upstream change about eagle
sglang.author: xiezhq-hermann
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
#3986 made substaintial change on memory pool which breaks hierarchical cache, this PR fix the interfaces problem around it.
There are separated PRs fixing other known issues with hierarchical caching, which will be merged later:
Large TP sizes: #4082
MLA support: #4009
Modifications

vllm.title: [V1][Core] Support for Structured Outputs
vllm.author: aarnphm
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR introduces the first iteration of structured output support for the V1 engine.
While functional, it is not feature complete with the support in V0. We currently only support xgrammar as a backend. We do not have a fallback in place to outlines as we did in V0. Other backends will come in a f

vllm.title: [Doc] Update prefix_caching.md to match the example image
vllm.author: York-RDWang
vllm.bodyExcerpt: In the case where the example image takes precedence, the current text description contains errors that may potentially lead to misunderstandings.

vllm.title: [Benchmarks] Make detokenization optional in benchmark scripts
vllm.author: JArnoldAMD
vllm.bodyExcerpt: The benchmark scripts currently include detokenization as part of the performance measurement.  In most cases the detokenization time is minimal, but for some models and script parameters it can be significant.  Since detokenization isn't always needed (e.g. it may be handled by a separate server),

vllm.title: [Kernel] optimize performance of gptq marlin kernel when n is small
vllm.author: jinzhen-lin
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The performance of gptq/awq marlin kernel is low when n is small. The reason is that when n is small, the barrier_acquire and barrier_release cost too many time.
The case where n is small often occurs during inference using larger tensor parallelism or in some special models. For example, in deepsee

vllm.title: [v1] torch.compile integration explanation
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt: As we roll out the v1 to the public, we need to explain how we use torch.compile

vllm.title: [V1] Eagerly remove finished requests from the batch
vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Currently, if all in-flight requests are aborted, the core engine loop will pause and only remove the completed requests from the batch the next time there are new requests to schedule.
We might as well do this early in this case so that it's not on the later critical path.
Other advantages of this:

vllm.title: [V1] Prompt logprobs + APC compatibility; prompt logprobs reqs cannot fill APC
vllm.author: afeldman-nm
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Fix the issue wherein APC must be disabled on a v1 engine instance in order to support requests with prompt logprobs enabled. Solve this issue through "near-full-recomputation" of prompt logprobs. See the discussion in #13414 for why full recomputation is desirable. A prompt logprobs request will no

Running
VLLM_USE_V1=1 vllm serve deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-Coder-V2-Lite-Instruct --tensor_parallel_size=2 --port 8192 --trust-remote-code

and then
lm_eval --model local-completions --tasks gsm8k --model_args model=deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-Coder-V2-Lite-Instruct,base_url=http://127.0.0.1:8192/v1/completions

vllm.title: [Bugfix][Disaggregated] Add a check in send_kv_caches_and_hidden_states and fix the reshape of the KVCache
vllm.author: hasB4K
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Hello,
This PR adds this in simple_connector.py::send_kv_caches_and_hidden_states:
       head_size = getattr(model_config, "head_dim",
                            int(hidden_size // num_attention_heads))

Indeed, the assumption that head_size == hidden_size // num_attention_heads is not always true

vllm.title: [MISC][V1] Register process killing handler only in the main thread
vllm.author: comaniac
vllm.bodyExcerpt: signal.signal can only be used in the main thread, or it will throw an exception otherwise. In the Ray Serve use cases, however, we launch an engine in a separate thread using asyncio. This PR makes signal.signal optional in engine core to workaround this issue, with a warning to let users know they

vllm.title: [core] add `extra_args` to `SamplingParams`
vllm.author: akeshet
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This PR adds an extra_args field to SamplingParams, for use by custom (out-of-tree) sampling implementations.

vllm.title: [CI/Build] refactor: set timezone of container to UTC
vllm.author: bufferoverflow
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Today the timezone of the container is set to America/Los_Angeles, this is confusing especially for logging purposes. This change does set the timezone to Etc/UTC which is typically used within container environments.
⚒️ with ❤️ by https://github.com/siemens

vllm.title: Default to `generation_config` from model
vllm.author: hmellor
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Changes the default behaviour of the feature introduced in #11164. The new behaviour for --generation-config:

auto (default) - generation config is loaded from model path
vllm - vLLM defaults are used
{directory} - generation config is loaded from directory

As discussed in https://vllm-dev.slack.c

Currently, there is little documentation for using vLLM in training scenarios.
This PR adds a training section to the index and a small page talking about the integration with TRL.
